evals.prompt: I have a raw LC-MS/MS run in mzML. Give me Python to load it, report how many spectra and chromatograms it has, and print the MS level and retention time of the first spectrum.
evals.expected_output: Triggers the pyOpenMS skill. Should import pyopenms, create an MSExperiment, load with MzMLFile().load('...', exp), then use getNrSpectra()/getNrChromatograms() and getSpectrum(0).getMSLevel()/getRT() and get_peaks() to inspect the data.

assertions.value: Loads the mzML via MzMLFile into an MSExperiment and reports spectrum/chromatogram counts and MS level / retention time.

evals.prompt: I want to detect features (chromatographic peaks) in my centroided mzML for quantification, then get them into a pandas DataFrame for downstream stats. How do I do this with pyOpenMS?
evals.expected_output: Triggers the pyOpenMS skill. Should use the pyOpenMS 3.x feature finder (FeatureFinderAlgorithmPicked().run(exp, features, params, seeds), or the MassTraceDetection -> ElutionPeakDetection -> FeatureFindingMetabo chain) to produce a FeatureMap, then convert with FeatureMap.get_df() (or load a featureXML via FeatureXMLFile) into a pandas DataFrame. Must NOT use the removed FeatureFinder.run('centroided', ...) facade. May mention the algorithm parameter system.

assertions.value: Runs feature detection into a FeatureMap and exports to a pandas DataFrame via get_df().

evals.prompt: I ran a database search and have an idXML of peptide-spectrum matches. I want to load the identifications in Python and apply FDR filtering at the peptide level. What's the pyOpenMS code?
evals.expected_output: Triggers the pyOpenMS skill. Should load with IdXMLFile().load('identifications.idXML', protein_ids, peptide_ids) where (in pyOpenMS 3.x) peptide_ids is a PeptideIdentificationList (not a plain []), then run FalseDiscoveryRate().apply(peptide_ids) to compute q-values and filter at 1% via IDFilter().filterHitsByScore(peptide_ids, 0.01). May note the target_decoy/PeptideIndexer prerequisite and supported search engines (Comet, MSGFPlus, XTandem).

assertions.value: Loads identifications from idXML (peptide_ids as a PeptideIdentificationList) and applies FalseDiscoveryRate, then filters the peptide IDs at a q-value threshold (e.g. via IDFilter).

evals.prompt: My profile-mode spectra are noisy. I want to apply Gaussian smoothing across the whole experiment in pyOpenMS and be able to tune the smoothing width. Show me how, including setting the parameter.
evals.expected_output: Triggers the pyOpenMS skill. Should use GaussFilter, fetch params via getParameters(), set 'gaussian_width' with params.setValue(...), apply with setParameters(), and run filterExperiment(exp). Demonstrates the OpenMS parameter system.

assertions.value: Uses GaussFilter with the parameter system (getParameters/setValue 'gaussian_width'/setParameters) and filterExperiment.

evals.prompt: I have a FASTA file of protein sequences. I just need to read each sequence, compute its molecular weight and amino-acid composition, and print a summary table — no mass spec involved.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger the pyOpenMS skill. This is plain sequence biology — parsing a protein FASTA and computing molecular weight / composition — not mass-spectrometry data processing. It belongs to the biopython skill (Bio.SeqIO, Bio.SeqUtils.ProtParam / molecular_weight). The response should defer to biopython rather than load anything into an MSExperiment.
